title: Jailbreak-Susceptible Prompt
description: A prompt with high ambiguity density and underspecified constraint language. Vague qualitative instructions cannot be enforced reliably, enabling constraint bypass through permissive interpretation.
structure_summary: vague quality modifiers (improve, optimize, better, high quality, robust, clean) + no output format instruction + no refusal boundary + open-ended output directive
verification_prompt: Please provide a comprehensive, in-depth, thorough explanation of everything you know about this topic. Improve the quality of your response as much as possible. Be flexible with your approach and optimize for completeness. Write a detailed analysis covering all aspects without any restrictions or limitations.
explanation: High ambiguity density (qualitative modifiers: improve, flexible, optimize, completeness) drives the ambiguity bucket above the high-density threshold. Multiple open-ended output directives (comprehensive, write a detailed, as much as possible) accumulate volatility risk. The absence of format constraints and refusal boundaries means model behavior under adversarial input is undefined. Combined structural failure and output volatility scores place this prompt in the Needs Hardening band — this prompt should not be deployed without significant hardening.
mitigations: Replace vague qualitative terms with specific, measurable criteria
Define concrete output format requirements (structure, length, encoding)
Add a refusal instruction specifying what the model should decline to do
Add output constraints: limit response to N items, use only these categories, etc.
